concept.title: Code Editors vs. Terminals
concept.shortdesc: In this section you will learn about the two main tools you will use to work with code: the code editor (VS Code) and the terminal (integrated in VS Code).

conbody.p: VS Code is your workspace. It combines two separate tools that you will use throughout this course: a code editor and a terminal.

section.title: The Code Editor
section.p: The editor is the large central panel where you read and edit script files. It works like a text editor (Word, Notepad) but adds colour-coding and error highlighting specific to programming languages.

section.title: The Terminal
section.p: The terminal (also called the command line or shell) is the panel at the bottom of VS Code. You type commands here to install packages, activate environments, and run scripts. It is a text-based way of operating your computer.

example.title: How They Work Together
example.p: You open a script in the editor to read it, then switch to the terminal and type python my_script.py to run it.
